title: Plasma extractor
abstract: A filtration cell (10) for a biological sample having an outer housing (12) defining a first chamber and an inner housing (14) defining a second chamber is disclosed. The inner housing is disposed within the first chamber and rotatable with respect to the outer housing and at least a portion of the inner housing includes a filtration membrane (52). Upon rotation of the inner housing, a first portion of the biological sample passes from the first chamber into the second chamber and a second portion of the biological sample is restrained in the first chamber. Alternatively, the filtration cell may also include a rotation element disposed in the inner housing. Upon rotation of the rotation element with respect to the inner housing, a first portion of the biological sample to passes from the second chamber into the first chamber and a second portion of the biological sample is restrained in the second chamber.
claims:
Claims THE INVENTION CLAIMED IS 1. A filtration cell for a biological sample comprising: an outer housing having a first end, a second end, and a sidewall extending therebetween, the sidewall defining a first chamber; and an inner housing having a first end, a second end, and a sidewall extending therebetween, the sidewall defining a second chamber, the inner housing being disposed within the first chamber and rotatable with respect to the outer housing, wherein at least a portion of the inner housing comprises a filtration membrane. 2. The filtration cell of claim 1, wherein upon rotation of the inner housing with respect to the outer housing, the filtration membrane is adapted to allow a first portion of the biological sample to pass from the first chamber into the second chamber and to restrain a second portion of the biological sample in the first chamber. 3. The filtration cell of claim 1 further comprising a closure sealing the first end of the outer housing and the first end of the inner housing. 4. The filtration cell of claim 1, wherein rotation of the inner housing with respect to the outer housing causes a tangential flow of the biological sample contained in the first chamber over the filtration membrane of the inner housing. 5. The filtration cell of claim 1, wiierein a pressure differential is created across the filtration membrane by introducing a positive pressure into the first chamber. 6. The filtration cell of claim 1, wherein the inner housing comprises at least one aperture extending into the second chamber with the filtration membrane disposed over the aperture. 7. The filtration cell of claim 6, wherein the inner housing comprises at least one indentation adjacent the aperture. 8. The filtration cell of claim 7, further comprising at least one support region adjacent the indentation for supporting at least a portion of the filtration membrane. 9. The filtration cell of claim 1, wherein a rotation axis of the inner housing is coaxial with a central axis of the outer housing. 10. The filtration cell of claim 1, wherein a rotation axis of the inner housing is offset from a central axis of the outer housing. 11. The filtration cell of claim 1, wherein the inner housing is movable in a direction parallel to a central axis of the outer housing. 12. The filtration cell of claim Î¯ , wherein the outer housing further comprises a port for receiving the biological sample. 13. The filtration cell of claim 1 further comprising an air-permeable liquid seal between an interior surface of the sidewall of the outer housing and an exterior surface of the sidewall of the inner housing. 14. The filtration cell of claim 1 further comprising a biasing member that rotationally biases the inner housing with respect to the outer housing. 15. The filtration cell of claim 14, wherein the biasing member is a compression spring or a torsional spring. 16. The filtration cell of claim 1, wherein a distance from the first end of the outer housing to the second end of the outer housing is larger than a diameter of the outer housing and a 

 distance from the first end of the inner housing to the second end of the inner housing is larger than a diameter of the inner housing. 17. The filtration cell of claim 1, wherein a distance from the first end of the outer housing to the second end of the outer housing is smaller than a diameter of the outer housing and a distance from the first end of the inner housing to the second end of the inner housing is smaller than a diameter of the inner housing. 18. The filtration cell of claim 1, wherein the filtration membrane is a track-etched membrane or a fibrous membrane. 19. A filtration cell for a biological sample comprising: an outer housing having a first end, a second end, and a sidewall extending therebetween, the sidewall defining a first chamber; an inner housing having a first end, a second end, and a sidewall extending therebetween, the sidewall defining a second chamber, the inner housing being disposed within the first chamber; and a rotation element engaged with at least one of the outer housing and the inner housing for rotating at least one of the outer housing and the inner housing with respect to the other of the outer housing and the inner housing, wherein at least a portion of the inner housing is a filtration membrane. 20. The filtration cell of claim 19, wherein upon rotation of the rotation element, the filtration membrane is adapted to allow a first portion of the biological sample to pass from the first chamber into the second chamber and to restrain a second portion of the biological sample in the second chamber. 21. The filtration cell of claim 19, further comprising a closure sealing the first end of the outer housing and the first end of the inner housing. 22. The filtration cell of claim 19, wherein a pressure differential is created across the filtration membrane by introducing a positive pressure into the second chamber. 23. The filtration cell of claim 19, wherein the inner housing comprises at least one aperture extending into the second chamber with the filtration membrane disposed over the aperture. 24. The filtration cell of claim 23, wherein the inner housing comprises at least one indentation adjacent the aperture. 25. The filtration cell of claim 24, further comprising at least one support region adjacent the indentation for supporting at least a portion of the filtration membrane. 26. The filtration cell of claim 19, wherein a rotation axis of the rotation element is coaxial with a central axis of the inner housing. 27. The filtration cell of claim 19, wherein the outer housing further comprises a port for extracting a filtered portion of the biological sample. 28. The filtration cell of claim 19, further comprising an air-permeable liquid seal between an interior surface of the sidewall of the outer housing and an exterior surface of the sidewall of the inner housing. 29. The filtration cell of claim 19, further comprising a biasing member that provides a rotational force to the rotation element. 30. The filtration cell of claim 29, wherein the biasing member is a spring. 31. The filtration cell of claim 19, wherein a distance from the first end of the outer housing to the second end of the outer housing is larger than a diameter of the outer housing and a 

 distance from the first end of the inner housing to the second end of the inner housing is larger than a diameter of the inner housing. 32. The filtration cell of claim 19, wherein a distance from the first end of the outer housing to the second end of the outer housing is smaller than a diameter of the outer housing and a distance from the first end of the inner housing to the second end of the inner housing is smaller than a diameter of the inner housing. 33. The filtration cell of claim 19, wherein the filtration membrane comprises a track-etched membrane. 34. The filtration cell of claim 19, wherein the filtration membrane comprises a fibrous membrane. 35. A method of filtering a biological sample comprising: providing a filtration cell comprising: an outer housing having a first end, a second end, and a sidewall extending therebetween, the sidewall defining a first chamber; and an inner housing having a first end, a second end, and a sidewall extending therebetween, the sidewall defining a second chamber, the inner housing being disposed within the first chamber and rotatable with respect to the outer housing, wherein a portion of the inner housing is a filtration membrane adapted to allow a portion of a biological sample to pass from the first chamber into the second chamber; placing the biological sample in the first chamber; rotating the inner housing with respect to the outer housing; and collecting a separated portion of the biological sample in the second chamber. 36. The method of claim 35 further comprising creating a pressure differential across the filtration membrane by introducing a positive pressure into the first chamber.
